headline: Trump 🍊🤝 MBS ☝️ Nuclear Deal ☢️🤯 Israel Cucked 💥
text: Trump 🍊👴 just YEETED 🗑️ decades of US policy by handing MBS ☝️ Saudi Crown Prince his nuclear dream ☢️ without forcing Israel 🤝 recognition! Past presidents made Saudi normalize relations 🕊️ with Israel 🇮🇱 before getting American nuclear tech 🔬 for civilian energy ⚡ but Trump said 'HOLD MY HAMBURGER 🍔' and gave Saudis what they WANTED 😩 without Israel concessions! Deal reportedly lets Saudi enrich uranium 🇸🇦💣 on their own soil 🌍 - that's a major W for MBS who said in 2018 'if Iran gets nukes, we get nukes too' ☢️🇮🇷😤. Critics calling it a 'GIVEAWAY' 🎁🤡 that could SPARK 🔥 nuclear arms race 🏎️💨 across Middle East 🌍 while US Energy Secretary Chris Wright claims it 'upholds highest safety standards' 🛡️😏. Deal also lets US companies like Westinghouse 💼🛠️ profit 💰 from building Saudi nuclear plants 🏭⚡ taking a decade to complete 🗓️. Israel politicians like Avigdor Lieberman screaming 'MAD ARMS RACE' 🏃‍♂️💥 while Dem congressman Ro Khanna called it 'NOT SMART POLICY' 🤦‍♂️. Meanwhile Trump 🍊 attended fallen service members' ceremony 🇺🇸 before Georgia rally claiming his policies make Middle East 'more stable than ever' 🌟🕊️. Transactional foreign policy 📈💼 hallmark of Trump 2.0 - America First 🇺🇸 while competing with China 🇨🇳 in nuclear tech race 🏆. Analysts say US basically telling Gulf allies 'you don't have to work with China, you can work with us' 🤝💼. MBS gets his nuclear flex ☢️💪 without recognizing Israel or dropping Palestinian state demands 🇵🇸 - diplomatic breakthrough with Israel now on BACK BURNER 🔥🍳. Analysts say deal 'lays groundwork for future nuclear weapons' 🔮☢️ even if Saudi doesn't race to build them right away 🐢. Nuclear power could help Saudi diversify from oil 🛢️💨 making energy 'self-contained' 🏠⚡ unlike oil tankers stuck in Hormuz strait 🚢🛑. Short-term political payoff for Saudi 💪🌟 while US companies get lucrative market 💰📈. Trump didn't even attend signing ceremony ✍️🚫 after dignified transfer ceremony for 4 US service members killed in Iran 🇺🇸💀. Deal reportedly lacks strict IAEA inspections like 2009 UAE agreement 📋❌. Saudi Arabia won't reap economic rewards for a decade 🕰️💸 but political W is IMMEDIATE 🚀. US ratcheting economic competition with China 🇨🇳 and Russia 🇷🇺 in Arctic 🧊 while signing Middle East nuclear pact ☢️🤝 raising tensions in volatile region 🌋. Transactional approach hallmark of Trump 2.0 - America First 🇺🇸 with 'America First' approach to global affairs 🌍💼. Deal appears to be latest example of Trump jettisoning traditional American approach to Middle East 🌍🚫 which centered on Israel and two-state solution 🕊️🇵🇸. Analysts say nuclear pact signals US wants to keep regional allies in fold 🤝 even if it means angering Israel in process 😤🇮🇱. Countries in Middle East want to develop nuclear infrastructure 🏗️☢️ and they're going to do it even if US not involved 🌍🚫. Washington recognizing that fact 🤔💡. Deal reportedly conditioned on Saudi joining Abraham Accords 🕊️📜 but unclear if stipulated in text or if Saudi agreed 🤷‍♂️. Deal differs from UAE 2009 agreement which included strict IAEA inspections 🔍📋. Critics pointing to uranium enrichment allowance on Saudi soil 🇸🇦💣. Even if Saudi doesn't develop weapons immediately, potential ability gives powerful deterrent force 🛡️💪. Oil can be disrupted 🚫🛢️, bombed 💥, tankers stuck 🚢🛑 but nuclear power self-contained 🏠⚡. Saudi Arabia already powerful Gulf state further cements status as major regional force 💪🌟. Deal allows Saudi to diversify energy sector ⚡💼 and meet rising electricity demands 📈. Having nuclear power could lessen oil reliance 🛢️➡️⚡ insulating from global oil market volatilities 🌍📉. American companies benefit from nuclear deal though not overnight 🕰️💰. Saudi will work with US-based firms 🏢🤝 to develop civilian nuclear technology 🔬⚡. Westinghouse manufactures large-scale conventional nuclear reactors 🏭⚛️. Timing of announcement not clearly explained 🤔🕐. War with Iran likely played part 💥🇮🇷. Washington sought to ease tensions with Saudi and Gulf allies targeted by Tehran 🎯. Saudi briefly denied US access to airspace earlier in war ✈️🚫. Saudi pushed US and Iran to de-escalate 🕊️🤝. Deal facing criticism in Israel and US 😤🇺🇸. Israeli politicians argue Saudi could become new nuclear threat ☢️⚠️. Israel widely believed to have nuclear weapons though not publicly acknowledged 🤫☢️. Israel joined US war with Iran over shared concerns about Tehran's nuclear program 🇮🇱🇺🇸🇮🇷. Avigdor Lieberman called deal 'mad arms race' 🏃‍♂️💥. Democratic lawmakers raised similar concerns 🤦‍♂️. Ro Khanna said deal 'giveaway to Saudis' 🎁🇸🇦 and 'only going to fuel further ambitions from Iran' 🇮🇷🔥. Deal 'destabilize region with Israel' 🇮🇱💥. 'Just not smart policy' 🤷‍♂️. Chris Wright said agreement would boost American economy 📈🇺🇸 and strengthen national security 🛡️. Wright said pact would 'uphold highest standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation' 🔒☢️. Administration fueled skepticism by not immediately releasing more information 🤔📋. Trump didn't preside over signing ceremony ✍️🚫. Trump attended dignified transfer ceremony for 4 US service members killed in Iran 🇺🇸💀 before Georgia rally arguing policies making Middle East 'more stable than ever' 🌟🕊️.
